description: Hardcoded list of built-in TypeAdapterFactories violates OCP.
reasoning: The `createFactories` method explicitly adds a fixed list of `TypeAdapterFactory` instances for various types. If Gson needs to support a new built-in type or a new way of handling an existing type, this method must be modified to add the new factory. This makes the class closed for extension without modification.

description: Conditional logic for date type adapters based on configuration violates OCP.
reasoning: The `addDateTypeAdapters` method uses `if-else if` statements to determine which date format (pattern-based or style-based) to use and whether to add SQL date type adapters. Adding a new date format or a new SQL date type would require modifying this method, making it not open for extension.

description: Conditional logic and hardcoded checks for specific Number subclasses in `value(Number)` and `alwaysCreatesValidJsonNumber` methods violate OCP.
reasoning: The `value(Number)` method and its helper `alwaysCreatesValidJsonNumber` contain `if-else if` chains and direct class comparisons (`c == Integer.class || c == Long.class || ...`) to determine if a `Number` subclass always produces a valid JSON number string. If a new `Number` subclass were introduced that also always produces a valid JSON number, these methods would need to be modified, violating OCP.
